The classification of Gochnatia oligocephala is further divided into its kingdom, which is Plantae, and its order, which is Asterales. intro: Gochnatia oligocephala, also known as the "tree daisy," is a plant species native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gochnatia oligocephala include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
